text: whether they can access it. If any of the member's custom roles grant the feature, they have it. User-level setting : If the feature is granted at the role level, it's available unless the member has disabled it in their own settings. User-level setting : If the feature is granted at the role level, it's available unless the member has disabled it in their own settings. The key takeaway: the organization-level toggle is a main switch. Custom roles are the per-member switches underneath it. A feature must be enabled at the organization level before custom roles can control who gets access. Available capabilities Each custom role can grant or restrict access to the following capabilities: Capability Capability Description Description Chat Chat Access to chat on web, desktop, and mobile apps Access to chat on web, desktop, and mobile apps Claude Cowork Claude Cowork Access to the Cowork agent mode Access to the Cowork agent mode Claude Code Claude Code Access to Claude Code Access to Claude Code Claude Design Claude Design Access to Claude Design to generate design artifacts Access to Claude Design to generate design artifacts Fast-mode models (Claude Code) Fast-mode models (Claude Code) Access to faster model options Access to faster model options Code execution and file creation Code execution and file creation Ability to run code and create files in conversations Ability to run code and create files in conversations Web search Web search Ability to use web search in conversations Ability to use web search in conversations Memory Memory Ability to use memory across conversations Ability to use memory across conversations Note: Chat is enabled by default for all custom roles, including ones created before this capability was added. If you want to restrict chat for a specific role, toggle it off when editing the role. Create a custom role Navigate to Organization settings > Custom roles . Navigate to Organization settings > Custom roles . Click “+ Add role” Click “+ Add role” Enter a name for the role (for example, "Developer," "Standard Access," or "Restricted"). Enter a name for the role (for example, "Developer," "Standard Access," or "Restricted"). Select the groups you want to assign to the role. Select the groups you want to assign to the role.
